errors: The ELM implementation has an age threshold mismatch. The CPG specifies an age range of 30-85 years, but the ELM implementation uses an age range of 30-80 years. The LDL Cholesterol threshold of 190 mg/dL matches the CPG requirements.

The HbA1c Laboratory Test value in the ELM implementation is specified as >= 9 g%, whereas the CPG requirements specify it as >= 6.5%. This is a mismatch between the ELM implementation and the CPG requirements.

The ELM implementation of the MeetsInclusionCriteria logic is incorrect. The CPG requires that all of the following must be true: Screening Age (age >= 40 years), AND Gender (female), AND Mammogram (no procedure in past 1 year). However, the ELM implementation uses an OR operator instead of an AND operator for the Screening Age and Gender conditions. The correct ELM implementation should be: [MeetsInclusionCriteria] = [Screening Age] AND [Gender] AND [Mammogram].

The ELM implementation has a logical error in the "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" definition. According to the CPG, screening is needed if the patient is 65 or older AND no falls risk assessment has been performed in the past 1 year. However, the ELM implementation defines "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" as [In Demographic] AND [Has Recent Falls Assessment], which means screening is needed if the patient is 65 or older AND a falls risk assessment has been performed in the past 1 year. This is the opposite of the CPG requirement. The correct definition should be [In Demographic] AND NOT [Has Recent Falls Assessment].

The ELM implementation of the MeetsExclusionCriteria logic is incorrect. The CPG specifies that a patient meets the exclusion criteria if they have a confirmed active Connective Tissue Disorder OR are on active amiodarone therapy. The ELM implementation references a different value set for "Alcohol Use Screening" (2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.3.1278) than the one specified in the CPG (2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1542). This discrepancy may affect the accuracy of the screening results. Additionally, the CPG specifies that no screening is needed if an alcohol misuse screening has been performed within the past 1 year with a result present, but the ELM implementation only checks for the existence of a screening within the past year, without considering the presence of a result.
